content: We can reframe planner module task decomposition as an intrinsically curious self-splitting process, where the ICM curiosity mechanism drives the planner to split undone tasks into finer-grained sub-tasks *only when the current task decomposition leaves high uncertainty about the outcome*, instead of following a fixed pre-set decomposition hierarchy. This creates a dynamic task decomposition that adapts to the agent’s current knowledge gap rather than human-imposed task structures.
source_topics: Planner module design for task decomposition
trigger_topic: Planner module design for task decomposition
